name: Steps to Take After a Delivery Driver Accident: A Road Map to Benefits
description: Steps to Take After a Delivery Driver Accident: A Road Map to Benefits - Boise Idaho

step.name: Step 1
step.text: How to File a Successful Claim as a Delivery Driver

step.name: Step 2
step.text: The gig economy has changed the landscape of workers' compensation. Companies like DoorDash, Amazon Flex, and Uber Eats often try to distance themselves from injured drivers. If you’ve been hurt while delivering, follow these steps to protect your rights.

step.name: Step 4
step.text: Evidence is king. The moment a crash or injury occurs, take a screenshot of your app. You need to prove you were in the 'active' phase of a delivery—either en route to a pickup or heading to a drop-off. If you’re 'off-app' or just cruising for orders, the company's liability significantly decreases.

step.name: Step 6
step.text: Do not 'tough it out.' Go to an Urgent Care or ER and tell them clearly: 'I was injured while performing a delivery.' This creates a medical record that links the injury to your work. If you wait a week, the insurance company will claim you hurt yourself at the gym or while moving furniture.

step.name: Step 8
step.text: This is where it gets tricky. You might be covered by:

step.name: Step 11
step.text: • Your own personal car insurance (only if you have a commercial rider).

step.name: Step 13
step.text: • State-mandated workers' compensation (if a judge rules you were actually an employee).

step.name: Step 15
step.text: Step 4: Report to the Platform and Your Employer

step.name: Step 16
step.text: Follow the app’s internal reporting procedure immediately. In Utah, you generally have a 180-day window, but for delivery drivers, we recommend reporting within 24 hours. The faster the report, the harder it is for them to deny the claim.

step.name: Step 18
step.text: Because gig platforms fight tooth and nail to maintain 'independent contractor' status, you likely won't get a fair shake on your own. A lawyer can evaluate if the company exerted enough control over you to be considered an 'employer' under Utah or Idaho law. Call Just Work Comp Law at 801-746-0290 to discuss your specific delivery accident.
